children.heading: Duration of support
children.label: a Duration of support
children.text: The period during which payments are made to a public entity from a grant under may not exceed 6 fiscal years.section 290ff(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: provide technical assistance to the entity regarding the process of submitting to the Secretary applications for grants under ; andsection 290ff(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: provide to the entity training and technical assistance with respect to the planning, development, and operation of systems of care described in .section 290ff–1 of this title

children.heading: Authority for grants and contracts
children.label: 2 Authority for grants and contracts
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical assistance under subsection (a) directly or through grants to, or contracts with, public and nonprofit private entities.

children.heading: Evaluations and reports by Secretary
children.label: c Evaluations and reports by Secretary

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall, directly or through contracts with public or private entities, provide for annual evaluations of programs carried out pursuant to . The evaluations shall assess the effectiveness of the systems of care operated pursuant to such section, including longitudinal studies of outcomes of services provided by such systems, other studies regarding such outcomes, the effect of activities under this part on the utilization of hospital and other institutional settings, the barriers to and achievements resulting from interagency collaboration in providing community-based services to children with a serious emotional disturbance, and assessments by parents of the effectiveness of the systems of care.section 290ff(a) of this title

children.heading: Report to Congress
children.label: 2 Report to Congress
children.text: The Secretary shall, not later than 1 year after the date on which amounts are first appropriated under subsection (c), and annually thereafter, submit to the Congress a report summarizing evaluations carried out pursuant to paragraph (1) during the preceding fiscal year and making such recommendations for administrative and legislative initiatives with respect to this section as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.
The term “child” means an individual through the age of 21 years.

children.label: A
children.text: the legal guardian of the child; and

children.label: B
children.text: as appropriate regarding mental health services for the child, the parents of the child (biological or adoptive, as the case may be), kinship caregivers of the child, and any foster parents of the child.

The term “funding agreement”, with respect to a grant under to a public entity, means that the Secretary may make such a grant only if the public entity makes the agreement involved.section 290ff(a) of this title
The term “serious emotional disturbance” includes, with respect to a child, any child who has a serious emotional disorder, a serious behavioral disorder, or a serious mental disorder.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: e Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this part shall be construed as limiting the rights of a child with a serious emotional disturbance under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act [ et seq.].20 U.S.C. 1400

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 1 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out this part, there are authorized to be appropriated $125,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.

children.heading: Limitation regarding technical assistance
children.label: 2 Limitation regarding technical assistance
children.text: Not more than 10 percent of the amounts appropriated under paragraph (1) for a fiscal year may be expended for carrying out subsection (b).
